name: org.machanism.machai:ghostwriter
description: Machai Ghostwriter is a Java-based command-line documentation and automation engine that scans a project directory tree and executes GenAI-powered workflows over matching files. It supports two main processing modes: (1) Guidance mode, which uses pluggable file-format reviewers (Java, Markdown, HTML/XML, TypeScript, Python, PlantUML, and a special @guidance.txt convention) to extract embedded @guidance: directives from files and build per-file prompt fragments; and (2) Act mode, which runs predefined TOML-based prompt templates ("acts") loaded from built-in classpath resources (/acts/*.toml) and optionally a user-provided directory or URL, with support for inheritance via basedOn. The engine provides filesystem-only traversal (no builds/dependency resolution), multi-module child-first processing using a project layout model, configurable excludes for common build/tooling directories and custom patterns, optional glob/regex path matching for targeted scanning, and optional multi-threaded module processing. For each processed file or directory, Ghostwriter composes prompts that include a structured project layout description (OS, project/module identifiers, source/test/docs folders, modules list, and project-relative file path) and applies system instructions plus task-specific guidance or act inputs, then invokes a configured GenAI provider/model via a provider manager with optional function tools. It can log composed provider inputs per file into a .machai/docs-inputs area for auditing. The project integrates with Machai components such as project-layout (module discovery and relative path computation), genai-client (provider orchestration), and a function-tools loader, and exposes a CLI with options for model selection, scan patterns, excludes, instructions, default guidance, act execution, concurrency, and input logging.

constructors.description: Creates the Ghostwriter CLI orchestrator with a configured AIFileProcessor and a required GenAI provider/model identifier (e.g., OpenAI:gpt-5.1). The CLI is responsible for parsing options, loading configuration, selecting processor mode (GuidanceProcessor or ActProcessor), and running scans.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.AIFileProcessor processor = new org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor(new java.io.File("."), "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", new org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator());
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.Ghostwriter gw = new org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.Ghostwriter("OpenAI:gpt-5.1", processor);
examples.description: Create a Guidance-mode processor and run Ghostwriter orchestration programmatically.

constructors.description: Creates a processor that scans the filesystem, uses ServiceLoader-discovered reviewers to extract @guidance directives per supported file type, and dispatches prompts to the configured GenAI provider. Supports module processing, path matching, excludes, and optional default prompt behavior.

examples.description: Construct a GuidanceProcessor with a root directory, provider/model id, and configurator.

constructors.description: Creates a processor that executes TOML-based act templates (built-in and/or user-defined) against matching files and directories. Acts can define instructions, inputs template, concurrency/excludes/nonRecursive settings, and inherit via basedOn.

examples.description: Construct an ActProcessor and select an act to apply as defaults.

features.name: scanDocuments
features.signature: void scanDocuments(java.io.File projectDir, java.lang.String scanDir) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Scans a project root using either a raw relative directory name or a glob:/regex: path matcher, configures matching semantics, and triggers filesystem traversal and processing. If scanDir is a directory, it is resolved relative to the project root and converted to a glob pattern; matching is applied to project-relative paths and excludes common build/tooling directories.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.AIFileProcessor processor = new org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor(new java.io.File("."), "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", new org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator());
processor.scanDocuments(new java.io.File("."), "glob:**/*.java");
examples.description: Scan only Java source files using a glob matcher.

features.name: Act loading with inheritance
features.signature: static void loadAct(java.lang.String name, java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object> properties, java.lang.String actsLocation) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Loads a TOML act definition from both a user-defined directory/URL and built-in classpath resources, supports basedOn inheritance, and merges dotted TOML keys into a properties map. Custom acts can override built-in acts, enabling extension and modification.

examples.snippet:
java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object> props = new java.util.HashMap<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object>();
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.ActProcessor.loadAct("help", props, "./acts");
examples.description: Load an act named 'help' and merge inherited properties.

features.name: Guidance extraction via Reviewer SPI
features.signature: java.lang.String parseFile(java.io.File projectDir, java.io.File file) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Selects a Reviewer implementation based on file extension (loaded via java.util.ServiceLoader) and extracts @guidance prompt fragments for supported formats. Returns null for unsupported files, directories, or when no guidance is present.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor gp = new org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor(new java.io.File("."), "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", new org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator());
java.lang.String fragment = gp.parseFile(new java.io.File("."), new java.io.File("src/main/java/MyClass.java"));
examples.description: Extract a guidance fragment from a Java source file if it contains @guidance: directives.

features.name: Provider prompt orchestration
features.signature: java.lang.String process(org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout projectLayout, java.io.File file, java.lang.String instructions, java.lang.String prompt) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Creates a GenAI provider using the configured model, applies registered function tools, sets working directory, composes a prompt with project layout metadata plus task/guidance text, optionally logs inputs to per-file logs, and performs the provider request.

examples.description: Send a composed prompt to the configured provider for a specific file.

features.name: Built-in act listing tool
features.description: Function tool registered into the provider that lists available built-in act templates found under /acts/*.toml in the Ghostwriter distribution (jar/zip), including a short description per act.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.ActFunctionTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.ActFunctionTools();
// Applied via org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader into a provider; tool name: "build_in_list_acts"
examples.description: Expose act discovery to the GenAI provider as a callable tool.
